questions.text: You are a PO at SBI. A customer Mr. Ramesh Gupta has complained that his net banking access has been blocked for 3 days. Write a formal email explaining the reason (scheduled maintenance) and the resolution timeline.
questions.modelAnswer:
To: ramesh.gupta@email.com
Subject: Resolution of Net Banking Access Issue

Dear Mr. Gupta,

I sincerely apologise for the inconvenience caused due to the disruption in your net banking services.

The temporary suspension was due to scheduled server maintenance from 14th to 16th June 2026. Your net banking access has been restored. Please reset your password if you face any difficulty.

For assistance, contact 1800-11-2211.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Name]
Probationary Officer, SBI

questions.text: You are a PO at a semi-urban SBI branch. An elderly woman, Mrs. Kamla Devi (72), reports that she shared her OTP with someone posing as SBI customer care and ₹45,000 was debited. She is in tears. Describe the steps you will take immediately.
questions.modelAnswer:
I would first calm Mrs. Kamla Devi and offer her a seat. My immediate priority would be to block her debit card through the bank system and help her call 1800-11-2211 to report the fraud.

I would assist her in filing a written complaint at the branch with all details of the transaction. I would also guide her to file a cybercrime complaint at cybercrime.gov.in.

I would explain the RBI Zero Liability policy, which protects customers from unauthorised transactions if reported within three working days. Finally, I would educate her about never sharing OTPs with anyone, including bank staff.

questions.text: You recently participated in a Financial Literacy Camp in the village of Rampur on 5th June 2026. Write a formal report to your Branch Manager covering objectives, activities, attendance, outcomes and recommendations.
questions.modelAnswer:
REPORT ON FINANCIAL LITERACY CAMP — RAMPUR VILLAGE

Date: 6th June 2026
To: The Branch Manager, SBI
Subject: Financial Literacy Camp — 5th June 2026

A Financial Literacy Camp was organised at Gram Panchayat Hall, Rampur on 5th June 2026. The camp covered PMJDY accounts, RuPay card benefits, UPI usage, and banking fraud awareness.

180 villagers attended including 110 women. 42 new PMJDY accounts were opened and 8 RuPay cards activated.

Recommendation: Monthly camps should be organised targeting remaining hamlets and a dedicated Business Correspondent should be deployed.

Submitted by:
[Your Name] | PO

questions.text:
Read the passage and write a precis in ~100 words.

"Financial inclusion remains one of India's most pressing challenges. Despite PMJDY bringing millions into formal banking, merely opening accounts is not enough. Active usage for credit, savings, insurance and remittances is the real measure of inclusion. Financial literacy, digital infrastructure, and trust in banking institutions must be strengthened simultaneously. Without addressing these interconnected challenges, universal financial inclusion will remain elusive."
questions.modelAnswer: Financial inclusion remains a major challenge in India. While PMJDY has brought millions into the banking system, account ownership alone is insufficient. True inclusion means active usage for savings, credit, insurance, and transfers. Achieving this requires improving financial literacy, expanding digital infrastructure, and building trust in banks. Unless these challenges are addressed together, universal financial inclusion will remain difficult to achieve.

questions.text: You are a customer of Punjab National Bank. You have been wrongly charged a processing fee of ₹2,500 for a loan you did not apply for. Write a formal letter to the Branch Manager requesting a refund and explanation.
questions.modelAnswer:
To,
The Branch Manager
Punjab National Bank
[Branch Address]

Date: 10th June 2026

Subject: Request for Refund of Wrongly Charged Processing Fee — Account No. XXXX1234

Respected Sir/Madam,

I am writing to bring to your attention an erroneous debit of ₹2,500 from my savings account (No. XXXX1234) on 8th June 2026, shown as a loan processing fee. I wish to clarify that I have not applied for any loan with your branch, nor have I authorised any such transaction.

I request you to kindly investigate this matter at the earliest and arrange for an immediate reversal of ₹2,500 to my account. I also request a written explanation of how this charge was applied without my consent.

I trust that this matter will be resolved promptly. Please feel free to contact me at [mobile number].

Yours faithfully,
[Your Name]
[Account Number]
[Contact Details]

typingParas: Success comes from consistent effort, not sudden motivation. Every day offers a chance to improve skills, learn something new, and move closer to goals through patience, discipline, and persistence.
